summary: The posts report on the ongoing use of small UAS by both sides, with the Russian "Rubikon" center claiming 103 targets destroyed on March 4 and 191 on March 3, including strikes on Ukrainian forces, FPV air defense against Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, and destruction of a 2S1 Gvozdika self-propelled howitzer. Ukrainian sources highlight the importance of antenna rotation systems for electronic warfare and video interception, specifically mentioning the Chinese PTS-303 system used by Russian forces. There is also evidence of Ukrainian efforts to conceal antennas, with one post showing a plastic mesh-based design to evade aerial reconnaissance. Additionally, an FPV drone narrowly missed a Russian Mi-8 helicopter, underscoring the persistent threat to rotary-wing assets.
